description:
Goal
Publish a stable compiler-facing fact surface for compiler2 call edges so fixtures can assert meaning without depending on allocator-order FnIds or synthetic function numbering.

Signal
- compiler2 can expose call-edge facts and synthetic target labels that remain stable across harmless internal id drift
- generated clause/continuation/lambda targets are identified by provenance or owner-local canonical labels rather than raw numbering
- optional snapshot rendering is derived from the same structured facts

Strategy
1. Identify the authoritative provenance already present for source functions, clauses, continuations, and lambdas.
2. Build one canonical target-labeling function from that authority.
3. Publish structured call-edge records plus a canonical renderer on top of those records instead of rendering raw SpecKeys directly.

Validation
- the five current outcome fixtures render semantically equivalent stable labels across reruns
- no canonical fixture-facing output includes raw allocator-order ids as its identity source

Removal targets
- render_dispatch_target dependence on raw FnId numbering for compiler contract assertions
- exact-text identity churn caused by synthetic k_### / lambda_### numbering
title: Publish canonical compiler2 call-edge facts and stable synthetic labels
